metadata.purpose: Persist non-pixel measurements and calculations behind approximate public source-photo footprints. A reproducible calculation is not the same as an independently validated georegistration.

source_lobe_detection.method: OpenCV BGR to Lab; mask L greater than 180; 8-connected components; retain area at least 1000 pixels

reference_lobe_detection.method: grayscale crop x 980:1140, y 570:730; GaussianBlur 5x5 sigma 1.2; HoughCircles dp 1, minDist 20, param1 80, param2 18, radius 8:30; retain global x greater than 1050

affine_fit.interpretation: Three controls exactly determine the six affine parameters. The zero training residual is not an accuracy or validation statistic.

orientation_disambiguation.reference_row_detection_method: crop x 995:1160, y 410:1120; CLAHE clipLimit 2 tiles 8x8; Canny 40/100 L2; HoughLinesP rho 1 theta pi/720 threshold 35 minLineLength 55 maxLineGap 14; retain 65 to 115 degrees
orientation_disambiguation.limitation: The numeric comparison is reproducible, but interpreting the low-tier source-image axis as field rows remains an inference.

manual_road_controls.name: Kenagy Road and South Whiskey Hill Road
South Barlow Road and South Whiskey Hill Road

reference_to_wgs84.limitations: Road-center pixels were manually read rather than stored as reviewed annotations; the latitude anchor is rounded; no independent fourth ground checkpoint exists.

detector_sensitivity_envelope.interpretation: Conditional detector-sensitivity envelope, not a confidence interval. It does not cover a wrong lobe permutation, semantic axis misinterpretation, or manual road-control error.

source_report_controls.coordinate_text: 43 degrees 31.81 minutes N, 88 degrees 31.59 minutes W
source_report_controls.coordinate_confirmation: ICCRA report states that two GPS units confirmed the location
source_report_controls.persistent_scene_clue: The formation lies immediately north of the east-west County Road T West field boundary; the workshop/house remains across the road.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The two best-resolved approximately 42-foot circles span about 56 pixels in the source aerial image (12.8 m / 56 px = 0.229 m/px).
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The report explicitly captions the frame as facing north; source image top is therefore treated as north and right as east.

quality.limitations: The report does not say which point inside the three-circle group supplied the coordinate. The road/field boundary is a visual scale check, not a stored surveyed checkpoint, and aerial perspective is approximated locally.

source_report_controls.coordinate_text: 42 degrees 36.04 minutes N, 84 degrees 00.04 minutes W
source_report_controls.site_text: Wheat field at the corner of Mason and Burkhardt roads

image_axis_control.mapping: The source x-axis bearing is set to 123.1 degrees so the 124.4-degree image axis maps onto the report's bidirectional 67.5/247.5-degree E/NE-W/SW axis.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The 48-52 foot main circle spans approximately 132 pixels in the source aerial image (about 0.12 m/px).
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: Low-tier image-axis candidate rotated onto the report's documented E/NE-W/SW pathway axis.

quality.limitations: The source coordinate is precise enough to identify the surviving field, but the photograph lacks an independent persistent ground checkpoint. The image-axis match may follow crop rows rather than the pathway and must remain provisional.

source_report_controls.coordinate_text: 35 degrees 49.32 minutes N, 82 degrees 36.17 minutes W
persistent_scene_clues: Eller Creek 20-30 feet from the formation

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The reported approximately 57-foot oval spans about 76 pixels in the source aerial image (17.5 m / 76 px = 0.23 m/px).
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: Unresolved. North-up is used only as a neutral display convention until the creek/tree pattern is matched to historical orthophotography.

quality.limitations: The actual report coordinate and scale are useful, but the oblique frame is not yet oriented or projectively fit. Creek and tree relief introduce parallax; a 2005-era orthophoto match is required before promotion to a geometry registration.

source_report_controls.site_text: August 13 symbol east of North 41st Street and north of Townline Road on the contemporaneous investigator map
current_road_names: Camp Phillips Road / County Road X

visual_matches: Townline Road and Camp Phillips Road / County Road X crossing
curved drainage and field access margin
landmark_controls.candidate_center_basis: The August 13 symbol centroid from the contemporaneous investigator map after fitting Townline Road, North 41st Street, McIntosh Road, and Lahr Road controls, transferred into the matched USGS frame.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The mapped separation between the persistent road crossing and farm entrance is approximately 0.97 metres per source pixel; the frame is an ICCRA-linked USGS follow-up image.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The source frame is north-up and its east-west and north-south roads coincide with current county road centerlines and satellite imagery.

quality.limitations: The contemporaneous road map and persistent roads and farm scene support the candidate field and source-frame display geometry, but the circles are not unambiguously visible in the early-1998 image. The symbol-derived candidate center therefore retains 100 m uncertainty and is not an alignment target.

source_report_controls.site_text: The user matched the ICCRA aerial to the field south of the divided-highway corridor using the road and drive geometry, tree rows, farm boundaries, buildings, and curved access roads visible in the 2000 historical frame.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Wavra Farm, Salem / Silver Creek Falls, Marion County

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact projective conversion of the user-supplied Google Earth Pro GroundOverlay LatLonBox. The raw KML box and rotation are retained as the authoritative display geometry.

quality.limitations: The registration is user-authored and has no independent withheld checkpoint set. The source image is oblique and the KML deliberately applies unequal horizontal and vertical scale.

source_report_controls.site_text: The source scene is registered southwest of Rockville Road and Suisun Valley Road using three non-collinear road controls. The corrected footprint aligns the named intersection and both road approaches instead of forcing an unskewed two-point similarity fit.

controls.name: Rockville Road and Suisun Valley Road intersection

projective_display_transform.basis: Three-point affine registration from non-collinear persistent road controls: western Rockville Road, the Rockville Road/Suisun Valley Road intersection, and Suisun Valley Road south of the intersection. The affine model solves rotation, scale, shear, and unequal axis scale for the oblique frame.

quality.limitations: The three controls solve the affine transform exactly, so no residual can be estimated from them. No independent withheld checkpoint or same-date orthophoto is available, and relief/perspective outside the road-control triangle can still distort the frame.

source_report_controls.site_text: The source aerial is matched to the Solano Community College field using the tennis courts, campus buildings, parking geometry, arterial road, and exact field corner.

projective_display_transform.basis: Similarity transform from the source formation anchor and the persistent Solano Community College tennis-court control, published as four projective display corners for the oblique frame.

quality.limitations: The tennis-court target was read manually from current imagery and may carry roughly 15-30 m error; the source frame is oblique and lacks an independent checkpoint.

source_report_controls.site_text: The ICCRA investigation records the formation at 39 degrees 37 minutes 12.36 seconds north, 84 degrees 16 minutes 14.08 seconds west and documents its southeast position relative to Miamisburg Mound.

projective_display_transform.basis: The source frame was centered on the historical Google Earth coordinate recorded in the ICCRA investigation and oriented along the report's measured line toward Miamisburg Mound.

quality.limitations: The transform is reconstructed from the report coordinate, length, and mound alignment rather than a withheld multi-point ground-control set.

source_report_controls.site_text: The report distances place the formation south of the Hopewell enclosure, north of North Fork Paint Creek, and east of the power-line corridor; persistent field and farm landmarks constrain this scene.

projective_display_transform.basis: Manual four-point scene fit to the southern Hopewell earthwork boundary, Sulphur Lick corridor, North Fork Paint Creek, farm buildings, and the power-line corridor.

quality.limitations: The source image is oblique, the formation anchor is approximate to about 15 pixels, and no independent withheld checkpoint set validates the four corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The source aerial's triangular field, road edges, interstate corridor, and campus tree line match the Starr Commonwealth campus scene east of Albion.

projective_display_transform.basis: Manual scene fit to the triangular Starr Commonwealth field bounded by 26 Mile Road, Interstate 94, and the persistent campus tree line.

quality.limitations: The 2002 frame is low resolution and oblique; corner precision is limited and no independent withheld checkpoint set is available.

source_report_controls.site_text: The source report places the six-to-eight-acre soybean field off Diehl Road on Molex property and in the shadow of an apartment complex. The source photograph matches the surviving Iroquois Club building and north parking geometry, with corporate parking on the opposite side of the field.
source_report_controls.reported_context: apartment complex and corporate office buildings

controls.name: Iroquois Club north parking boundary east end
Iroquois Club north parking boundary west end

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact three-control affine display transform from the 310 by 178 source frame to the official Illinois 2005 Chicago Urban Area orthophoto. The controls are the two ends of the Iroquois Club north parking boundary and the northern office-parking corner visible at the opposite side of the field.

quality.limitations: The low-resolution source is strongly oblique. All three controls solve the affine transform, leaving no independent residual or held-out checkpoint; building height and outer corners may retain perspective error.

source_report_controls.site_text: The investigation places the formation on a tree-surrounded island in Paint Creek, at least one-half mile from a road and less than two miles from Seip Earthworks. The wide 2003 aerial independently matches Bainbridge, the creek loop, the island, and the Seip vicinity; the display frame matches the four persistent island boundaries.
source_report_controls.reported_landform: island in Paint Creek surrounded by a wide band of trees

visual_matches: creek channel immediately northwest of the island
Bainbridge town edge in the companion wide aerial
Seip Earthworks vicinity in the companion wide aerial

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 275 by 184 ICCRA frame to the surviving Paint Creek island boundary. The controls are four non-collinear intersections of the persistent creek-side tree line and the enclosed field boundary.

quality.limitations: All four controls participate in the exact projective solution, leaving no independent held-out ground checkpoint. Tree growth, river migration, low source resolution, and extrapolation near the outer frame limit corner precision.

source_report_controls.site_text: The formation was reported across from the Serpent Mound entrance on Ohio 73 and along Brush Creek. The ICCRA mapping slide shows the formation, road, creek, entrance, and mound in one frame; those controls resolve the field southwest of the entrance. The direct ICCRA aerial's visible road segment supports a provisional display fit in that field.
source_report_controls.reported_context: across from the entrance of Serpent Mound
source_report_controls.reported_diameter_range_ft: approximately 200 to 300 across the surviving reports

controls.name: formation center from ICCRA mapping aerial

visual_matches: field immediately southwest of the Serpent Mound entrance
Brush Creek crossing and tree corridor
direct formation-to-mound alignment shown in the ICCRA case PDF

projective_display_transform.basis: Road-aligned anisotropic affine display fit from the 274 by 183 ICCRA frame. The visible Ohio 73 segment is matched at two separated points, while the source-frame formation anchor is placed within the uniquely resolved field shown by the ICCRA mapping slide.

quality.limitations: The direct 274 by 183 frame is oblique and exposes only a short road segment. The formation anchor comes from the companion mapping aerial rather than a surveyed coordinate, the source reports disagree on overall diameter, and no independent held-out checkpoint validates scale or corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the field immediately northwest of Chesterton Windmill. The near-nadir source frame contains the formation, the windmill, and the distinctive persistent grass island surrounding the monument in one scene.

visual_matches: Chesterton Windmill tower and sail hub
distinctive hooked grass island surrounding the windmill
formation field immediately northwest of the monument
publisher coordinate at the formation center

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact three-control affine display transform from the 800 by 306 source frame to an Esri World Imagery export. Controls are the publisher-mapped formation center, the persistent Chesterton Windmill building centroid, and the northern windmill-island crop-boundary intersection.

quality.limitations: All three controls participate in the exact affine solution, leaving no independent held-out checkpoint. Seasonal crop tracks differ between the 2018 source and current imagery, and the outer source-frame corners retain perspective and source-coordinate uncertainty.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the arable field immediately southwest of Figsbury Ring. A wide source aerial contains the full formation and the complete persistent hillfort in one frame, uniquely resolving the field and scene orientation.

visual_matches: formation field immediately southwest of the hillfort
publisher coordinate at the formation center

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 800 by 449 source frame. The publisher-mapped formation center and three distributed points on the persistent Figsbury Ring earthwork constrain the field, rotation, perspective, and scale.

quality.limitations: The source is oblique and the hillfort has real terrain relief, so one planar transform cannot eliminate parallax across the full frame. The controls were manually selected, the held-out earthwork checkpoint has a 37 m residual, and no surveyed ground control validates the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher coordinate places the formation in the arable field southeast of Yarnbury Castle and south of the A303. The selected source aerial contains the complete formation, A303, and the full persistent earthwork in one frame, uniquely resolving the field and orientation.

visual_matches: A303 alignment between the earthwork and formation
formation field southeast of the hillfort
publisher coordinate at the formation center

projective_display_transform.basis: Exact four-control projective display transform from the 800 by 1139 source frame. The publisher-mapped formation center and three distributed points on the persistent Yarnbury Castle earthwork constrain the unique field, scene rotation, perspective, and scale.

quality.limitations: The source is oblique and the earthwork has terrain relief, so a planar transform cannot eliminate all parallax across the frame. Controls were manually selected and no surveyed ground control validates the footprint corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The exact publisher target places the 2018 Coneybury Hill formation in the arable field south of the A303. Same-flight wide aerials show the formation field, the distinctive paired copses, the A303 corridor, and the surrounding field pattern, resolving the unique field while the selected tight aerial preserves the formation geometry at useful resolution.

projective_display_transform.basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 501 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 40 metre diameter, and the outer-ring cardinal points constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials showing the paired copses and field context constrain scene orientation, but do not supply independent ground control for this tight frame.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials place the two persistent copses on the source horizontal axis; the tight frame's tramline direction is consistent with that scene orientation.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 40 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is inferred from same-flight scene context and field tramlines; no persistent ground point independently validates the projected corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The exact publisher target places the 2016 formation in the arable field immediately south of Cley Hill. Same-flight wide aerials show the hill earthworks, the hill-to-field boundary, and the formation field, resolving the unique field; the selected tight aerial preserves the formation geometry and tramline direction at useful resolution.

projective_display_transform.basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 542 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 43 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials identify the field immediately south of Cley Hill; the tight frame's tramlines align with the persistent field direction visible in reference imagery.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials resolve the formation field immediately south of Cley Hill. The tight frame's tramline axis is aligned to the matching persistent southeast field direction in the reference mosaic.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 43 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is inferred from same-flight context and matching field tramlines; no persistent ground point independently validates the projected corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher target places the 2019 formation in the arable field immediately south of Cocum Farm's long straight tree avenue. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the avenue, its southern hedge junction, surrounding field boundaries, road, and farm cluster, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant; the selected tight frame preserves the formation geometry.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Cocum Farm, near Barton Stacey, Hampshire

projective_display_transform.basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the tight 800 by 500 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 61 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials show the distinctive north-south tree avenue, adjoining hedgerows, road, and farm context, resolving the field and image-up orientation.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial places the long Cocum Farm tree avenue directly north of the formation and preserves the tight frame's star and tramline orientation, fixing image-up approximately to true north.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 61 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is transferred from same-flight context; no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2021 formation in the arable field immediately east of the large Kitt's Lane woodland block. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the woodland edge, nearby buildings, field corners, and hedge pattern, resolving the unique field; the selected near-nadir frame preserves the formation geometry.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Kitt's Lane, near West Meon, Hampshire

projective_display_transform.basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the near-nadir 800 by 800 aerial. The publisher's BNG map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 55 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials show the field beside the persistent woodland block, Kitt's Lane buildings, hedges, and field corners, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials match the woodland block, Kitt's Lane building cluster, hedgerows, and field corners; the near-nadir frame is rotated so image-up points approximately west-northwest toward that woodland context.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center uses the transformed BNG coordinate; the Streetmap URL's human-readable latitude/longitude label differs from that BNG target. The 55 metre size is reported rather than surveyed, orientation is transferred from same-flight context, and no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The exact publisher target places the 2019 formation in the arable field south of the A362 and north of Clear Wood. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the road corridor, long field edges, hedge junctions, woodland, and Cley Hill profile, resolving the unique field; the selected tight frame preserves the formation geometry.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Clear Wood, Cley Hill, near Warminster, Wiltshire

projective_display_transform.basis: Four-point projective crop-geometry rectification of the 800 by 506 aerial. The publisher map target fixes the center, the source report documents a 55 metre diameter, and four outer-ring controls constrain local scale and perspective. Same-flight wide aerials show the A362 corridor, Clear Wood edge, field corners, hedges, and Cley Hill context, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials resolve the road, Clear Wood, field corners, and Cley Hill context. The selected frame's bubble axis points toward the road side of the field, fixing image-up approximately north within the stated uncertainty.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty; the 55 metre size is reported rather than surveyed; orientation is transferred from same-flight context; no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2016 formation in the large arable field immediately west of Etchilhampton village. The same-flight wide aerial reproduces the field outline, village, eastern road, northern hedge, adjoining fields, and crop-row direction, resolving the unique field; the selected near-nadir frame preserves the ring and central flattened-circle geometry.

projective_display_transform.basis: Four formation-extremity points on the near-nadir 800 by 735 aerial constrain local scale and orientation around the publisher's BNG target using the report's c.89 feet (24m) size statement. A same-flight wide aerial reproduces the uniquely shaped field, Etchilhampton village, the eastern road, northern hedge, and field-row direction, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant. This is a display placement, not an independently controlled perspective correction.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The same-flight wide aerial matches the uniquely shaped field west of Etchilhampton village, the eastern road, northern hedge, and adjoining fields. Its crop-row direction transfers the near-nadir frame so image-up points approximately west-northwest.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty. The report's 89-foot value converts to about 27.1 metres and conflicts with its 24-metre parenthetical; this display uses the stated 24-metre metric value and discloses that discrepancy. Orientation is transferred from same-flight context, and no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's ten-digit BNG target places the 2020 formation in the arable field beside the Ridgeway near Hackpen White Horse. The same-flight panorama reproduces the oval copse, Ridgeway track, road loop, white-horse pasture, and parcel edges and was matched to the selected tight frame with 511 robust local-feature inliers, resolving the field and yaw quadrant.
source_report_controls.reported_place: The Ridgeway, near Hackpen White Horse, Wiltshire

projective_display_transform.basis: Four formation-extremity points on the near-nadir 800 by 766 aerial constrain local scale and orientation around the publisher's ten-digit BNG target using the report's c.110 feet (33.5m) size. A same-flight panorama matched through 511 robust local-feature inliers reproduces the oval copse, Ridgeway track, road loop, white-horse pasture, and parcel edges, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant. This is a display placement, not an independently controlled perspective correction.

source_registration.orientation_basis: A same-flight panorama transfers the tight-frame tramline axis through 511 robust local-feature inliers and matches the oval copse, Ridgeway track, road loop, white-horse pasture, and parcel edges. The resulting undirected field axis is approximately 23.5 degrees true.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a landmark or survey georegistration. The atlas conservatively retains 25 metre center uncertainty despite the publisher's fine BNG formatting. The 33.5 metre size is reported rather than surveyed, panorama-to-tight-frame feature matching transfers orientation but not independent terrain control into the tight frame, and no persistent ground checkpoint independently validates the projected corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2021 formation in the arable field below Roundway Hill. Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the distinctive surrounding field network, southwest corner, hedges, and row direction, resolving the unique field and yaw quadrant; the selected near-nadir frame preserves the circular geometry and interior triangular pattern.

projective_display_transform.basis: Four formation-extremity points on the near-nadir 800 by 800 aerial constrain display scale and skew around the publisher's BNG target using the report's c.160 feet (49m) size. Same-flight wide frames reproduce the distinctive field network and southwest field corner, resolving the unique field and the approximately 83-degree true undirected row axis. The points are formation geometry rather than persistent ground controls.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Same-flight wide aerials reproduce the distinctive field network and southwest field corner. The selected frame's nearly horizontal tramlines transfer to an approximately 83-degree true undirected row axis in the north-up reference mosaic.

quality.limitations: This is a center, documented-size, and row-axis placement rather than a terrain homography or survey georegistration. The BNG x/y values are authoritative; the Streetmap URL's human-readable sv latitude/longitude label is inconsistent by about 111 metres. The center retains 25 metre uncertainty, the 49 metre diameter is reported rather than surveyed, the row direction is transferred from same-flight context, and no persistent ground point independently validates the tight-frame corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2016 Ansty formation in the small arable field at Ansty Farm. The source aerial and reference mosaic reproduce the adjacent plant nursery, road and farm compound, field boundaries, and isolated southeastern shed, resolving the unique field. The selected frame preserves both the full formation and those surrounding scene clues.

projective_display_transform.basis: Four formation-extremity points on the 800 by 600 near-nadir aerial constrain local scale and orientation around the publisher's BNG target using Temporary Temples' approximately 450-foot (137-metre) diameter. The nursery, road and farm compound, target-field boundaries, and isolated southeastern shed resolve the unique field and yaw quadrant. The shed contributes contextual orientation evidence and is not an independent checkpoint. This is not a complete terrain registration.

source_registration.orientation_basis: The source-to-reference vectors from the formation center to the persistent isolated southeastern shed, together with the nursery, road, compound, and field boundaries, orient image-up approximately 19 degrees east of true north.

quality.limitations: This is a crop-geometry placement, not a survey georegistration. The center inherits the publisher's 25 metre uncertainty. Temporary Temples reports about 450 feet (137 metres), while another secondary summary reports 110 metres, so display scale remains provisional. The contextual shed participates in the orientation choice, leaving no independent checkpoint; independently surveyed controls would be required before promotion.

source_report_controls.site_text: The publisher's BNG target places the 2016 Ranscomb Bottom formation in the distinctive hook-shaped arable field below the downs. Wider source frames reproduce both curved headlands, the bottom track, upper downland edge, scattered trees, and surrounding field junctions, resolving the unique field. The selected tighter aerial preserves the complete overlapping-pentagram geometry.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Ranscomb Bottom, near Calstone Wellington and Cherhill, Wiltshire

projective_display_transform.basis: Four formation-extremity points on the tight 800 by 605 aerial are scaled around the publisher's BNG target using the report's c.580-foot (177-metre) overall size. Wider source frames reproduce the unique hook-shaped field, bottom track, upper downland edge, curved headlands, and scattered trees, resolving the field and approximate row axis. The footprint is a coordinate-, size-, and orientation-constrained display placement rather than a landmark image-to-ground registration.

source_registration.orientation_basis: Wider aerials identify the unique hook-shaped field. The tight frame's tramlines are aligned to the approximate undirected row axis visible in the current reference field; image-up is estimated about 7 degrees west of true north.

quality.limitations: The four calculation points are formation geometry, not ground controls. The 177 metre overall size is reported rather than surveyed, the source is mildly oblique, orientation is transferred from field-row and wider-context evidence with a 180-degree ambiguity, and no persistent geographic checkpoint independently validates the image corners. The earlier four-boundary contextual trial missed the publisher center by about 37 metres and was rejected rather than promoted.

source_report_controls.site_text: The 2002 Darfield catalog diagram's ring of circles, central bullseye, and detached two-circle tail uniquely match the north-up historical frame. The frame's road offset favors the northern of two legacy coordinates as the display anchor; the points remain separate evidence records because they disagree by 46.543 metres.
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The 2002 catalog diagram exactly matches the historical frame; the distinct 2001 Darfield star/mandala diagram does not.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The source frame's 30 metre Google Earth scale bar spans 303 pixels from x=1614 to x=1917 at y=1077, giving 0.09900990099 metre per pixel.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The publishing page states that every displayed Google Earth frame is north-up; image x therefore points east and image y points south. This is publisher metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

quality.limitations: This is a legacy-coordinate, measured-scale, publisher-orientation display placement, not a survey or landmark georegistration. The two legacy points disagree by 46.543 metres; the source frame's Google Earth acquisition date is not stated; current road and field appearance may differ; and zero independent ground checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The source photograph and historical Google Earth frame crosswalk to the Barton Hills formation near Hexton. A distinct 2002 Lilley/Telegraph Hill radial formation was reviewed and rejected as the image match.
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The case-specific corn558 photograph and ENHE200202 historical frame match the Barton Hills near Hexton formation; the distinct Lilley/Telegraph Hill radial diagram does not.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The source frame's 30 metre Google Earth scale bar spans 346 pixels from x=1572 to x=1918 at y=1077, giving 0.086705202312 metre per pixel.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The publishing page states that every displayed Google Earth frame is north-up; image x therefore points east and image y points south. This is publisher metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

quality.limitations: This is a legacy-coordinate, measured-scale, publisher-orientation display placement, not a survey or landmark georegistration. The source acquisition date is not stated, the canonical event date conflicts with the case-specific date, and zero independent ground checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The legacy jingjang description, Waden Hill locality, catalog diagram, and historical frame identify the 2002-06-21 Waden Hill event. The original KML folder's old-or-iffy label remains part of the provenance.

source_report_controls.legacy_description_match: jingjang cropcircle close to Avebury/Waden Hill
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The legacy yin-yang description, the 2002-06-21 Waden Hill catalog diagram, and the sixth 2002 Wiltshire event code ENWI200206 agree.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The source frame's 30 metre Google Earth scale bar spans 351 pixels from x=1567 to x=1918 at y=1077, giving 0.085470085470 metre per pixel.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The publishing page states that every displayed Google Earth frame is north-up; image x therefore points east and image y points south. This is publisher metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

quality.limitations: This is a legacy-coordinate, measured-scale, publisher-orientation display placement, not a survey or landmark georegistration. The legacy source itself marks the record old or iffy, the historical acquisition date is not stated, and zero independent ground checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The historical thumbnail's three overlapping circles match the 2004 Panocchia reports and diagram; the distinct 2005 bullseye event was reviewed and rejected as the image match. The source map URL repeats the preferred coordinate.
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The 2004 reports and diagram show three chained 20 metre diameter circles matching the thumbnail; the 2005 event is a distinct bullseye.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: Three independently reported 20 metre diameter circles average 23.07 pixels across in the historical thumbnail, giving 0.8670520231 metre per pixel. This is a formation-size inference, not a map scale bar.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The 2006 Google Maps thumbnail and linked map view are presented north-up; image x is treated as east and image y as south. This is source presentation metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Historical Google Maps source presentation and linked map view

quality.limitations: The 160 by 120 pixel source has no persistent landmark controls and an unknown imagery acquisition date. Scale is inferred from reported formation size rather than a map scale bar, two legacy points disagree by 71.735 metres, and zero independent checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The legacy Dodworth point, distinctive pentagram-in-circle catalog diagram, corn550 ground photograph, and ENYS200201 historical frame crosswalk to the same St John the Baptist Church near Dodworth event.
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The PDF catalog's pentagram-in-circle diagram, corn550 ground photograph, CRA Dodworth code ENYS200201, and historical ghost agree.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The source frame's 30 metre Google Earth scale bar spans 336 pixels from x=1582 to x=1918 at y=1077, giving 0.089285714286 metre per pixel.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The publishing page states that every displayed Google Earth frame is north-up; image x therefore points east and image y points south. This is publisher metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

quality.limitations: This is a legacy-coordinate, measured-scale, publisher-orientation display placement, not a survey or landmark georegistration. The historical acquisition date is not stated, two sources disagree by two days on the event date, and zero independent ground checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The legacy Hackpen point, corn664 radial-wheel aerial, 2003-07-20 catalog diagram, and ENWI200321 historical ghost crosswalk to the same event. The distinct 2003-07-27 serpent design is rejected as the image match.
source_report_controls.legacy_folder: Crop Circle Collection / Old or iffy Crop Circles
source_report_controls.legacy_description_match: cropcircle cutted Hackpen Hill; linked corn664 aerial
source_report_controls.event_crosswalk_basis: The corn664 radial-wheel aerial, the 2003-07-20 Hackpen Hill catalog diagram, CRA code ENWI200321, and the historical ghost agree; the July 27 serpent does not.

local_display_transform.scale_basis: The source frame's 30 metre Google Earth scale bar spans 339 pixels from x=1580 to x=1918 at y=1077, giving 0.088495575221 metre per pixel.
local_display_transform.orientation_basis: The publishing page states that every displayed Google Earth frame is north-up; image x therefore points east and image y points south. This is publisher metadata, not an independently surveyed ground orientation.

source_registration.north_up_basis: Publishing-page statement that all displayed Google Earth images are north-up

quality.limitations: This is a legacy-coordinate, measured-scale, publisher-orientation display placement, not a survey or landmark georegistration. The legacy folder marks the placemark old or iffy, the source acquisition date is not stated, the formation center was read from a faint compressed ghost, and zero independent ground checkpoints validate the corners.

source_report_controls.site_text: The Diessenhofen field is identified by the Rhine, both river-bank alignments, the southern transport corridor, persistent tree lines, buildings, farm tracks, and the surrounding field boundaries.

landmark_controls.name: southeast field boundary at southern transport corridor
southwest field edge at southern transport corridor

projective_display_transform.basis: Manual correspondences on four persistent boundaries surrounding the formation field, checked against the Rhine, tree lines, buildings, farm tracks, and southern transport corridor in the wider source scene.

transform.basis: Manual correspondences on four persistent boundaries surrounding the formation field, checked against the Rhine, tree lines, buildings, farm tracks, and southern transport corridor in the wider source scene.

scene_identity_evidence: Rhine river channel and both bank alignments
road and building group north of the river
isolated tree cluster and green wedge immediately northwest of the formation field
west crop boundary and east farm-track boundary
southern railway/road corridor, including the train visible in the source frame

rights.author: Hansueli Krapf
rights.attribution: Hansueli Krapf / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0

quality.limitations: The transform was measured manually against an undated current mosaic. The source is oblique, distant portions of the frame are not coplanar with the formation field, field boundaries may have changed since 2008, and no withheld checkpoint set validates the corners. Corner uncertainty is therefore larger than anchor uncertainty.

source_report_controls.site_text: The frame depicts the same Diessenhofen formation and field during the same documented 2008-07-15 aerial sequence.

projective_display_transform.basis: Same-flight similarity transfer to the manually controlled Diessenhofen reference frame, constrained at the reviewed formation center; full-frame corners are extrapolated.

rights.author: Hansueli Krapf
rights.attribution: Hansueli Krapf / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0

quality.limitations: This same-flight frame uses a constrained SIFT similarity transfer (5 RANSAC inliers on its final edge) from the reviewed Diessenhofen reference frame. The formation center was visually checked, but oblique parallax, extrapolated frame corners, and the absence of independent ground checkpoints make the display warp provisional and exclude it from alignment calculations.

source_report_controls.site_text: The 2009-07-27 aerial and 2009-07-25 ground photograph document the Hörhausen formation discovered on 2009-07-12. Their geotags constrain the same Tägermoos field vicinity; the later aerial states that the formation was 15 days old.
source_report_controls.reported_place: Tägermoos between Steckborn and Hörhausen, Thurgau, Switzerland

projective_display_transform.basis: Four crop-geometry extremities in the oblique aerial were rectified to the documented 120 metre formation envelope. The source-row direction was aligned to the approximately 130 degree true field-row direction visible in current reference imagery. Full-frame corners are extrapolated from that display transform.

quality_gate.reason: crop geometry and approximate geotags are not independent persistent ground control

quality.limitations: The source frame is oblique and close-cropped, the two geotags may represent photographer or camera positions rather than surveyed formation control, no 2009 orthophoto was available in the public workflow, and zero independent persistent-landmark checkpoints validate the projected corners.
